title: Auxiliary Time-to-Stop Target Instead Of Binary-Only Training
summary: Recent F1 pit-stop papers report gains from predicting both next-lap stop and stop horizon.
rationale: Recent F1 pit-stop papers report gains from predicting both next-lap stop and stop horizon. This can regularize the model toward a smoother notion of strategic urgency than a single sparse binary label.
implementation_hint: Add an auxiliary target such as laps_until_next_pit within the current stint/race, censored at a small horizon like 5 to 8 laps. Test either a multi-output tabular NN, two-stage model where the regression output is fed to the classifier, or a stack of horizon-specific binary heads whose outputs are summarized back into next-lap probability.
expected_effect: Improved discrimination around borderline pit windows and fewer brittle threshold-like predictions.
risk: Constructing the auxiliary target incorrectly can leak future information; censoring and missing terminal cases must be handled carefully.
